scope: Triage of 20 failures remaining after bug-found-001/002/003 fixes shipped

windows-teardown-ebusy.description: Refined diagnosis: NOT discarded handles like bug-002. The test correctly closes its own DB; the leak was WAL/SHM sibling files lingering after openRuntimeDb (which enables WAL mode) in src/commands/context-health.js. Fix: added wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before db.close() in production code + cleanupTmpDir helper in afterEach as a safety net.
windows-teardown-ebusy.resolution: context-health 7/7 green. Full suite delta: 2407/2422 (was 2402/2422). 6 EBUSY recoveries, 1 flake offset elsewhere.

real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.description: ROOT CAUSE: live session keys carry the format `direct-session:{ts}:{agent}` with colons. NTFS reserves `:` in filenames — `mkdir 'direct-session:123:deyvin'` fails with ENOENT on Windows. The fs.mkdir calls inside writeLiveState/appendLiveEvent/writeLiveSummary were wrapped in `try { ... } catch { /* filesystem is auxiliary */ }` which silently swallowed the error, leaving state.json/events.ndjson/summary.md never written. All 6 failures stem from this — 2 read state.json directly (ENOENT), 1 relies on validation logic that reads state.json, 1 relies on listLiveStates scanning state.json files, and the others count derivative state that depends on the file existing.
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.evidence_pattern: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open 'C:\Users\...\aioson-live-command-*\.aioson\runtime\live\direct-session:1778803...:deyvin\state.json'
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.resolution: Added sessionKeyToDirName() helper in src/commands/live.js that replaces `:` with `__`. Applied in resolveLivePaths (single boundary point — all FS writes go through it). Exported the helper so tests can construct paths matching the on-disk layout. The session_key stored in SQLite / returned by CLI keeps its colons (it's a public identifier). live-command 8/8 + live-json-output 2/2 + runtime-command relevant test green. Full suite: 2413/2422 (was 2407/2422).
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.footnote: Feature was silently broken on Windows since commit 4621cf3 (feat: add tracked live sessions and runtime projections) — CI runs on Linux where colons are valid filenames so it never surfaced. Dashboard live view never worked on Windows before this fix.

test-needs-update-new-check-noise.description: Tests stub dossierTelemetry.emitDossierEvent and count calls. They were written before the active-learning-loop Phase 6 'learning_loop_template_parity_violation' check existed. The new check emits one extra event in both tested scenarios, breaking the count assertion.
files: tests/sync-agents-preflight.test.js:146:emits sync_agents_parity_violation when main() aborts on violation (expects 1 call, gets 2)
tests/sync-agents-preflight.test.js:194:main() returns 0 without emitting events when there are no violations (expects 0 calls, gets 1)
test-needs-update-new-check-noise.fix_recipe: Either (a) filter calls by payload.type before counting (only count sync_agents_parity_violation), or (b) add a second assertion accounting for the new template parity check. (a) is more surgical.

stale-fixture-path.description: Tests reference docs/en/schemas/index.json but neither docs/en/schemas/ nor docs/schemas/ exists in the repo. Either the schemas were never created in that location, or they were removed/relocated without updating the tests.
files: tests/json-schema-files.test.js:17:json schema index is valid and references existing schema files
tests/json-schema-files.test.js:65:json schema files expose required metadata
stale-fixture-path.fix_recipe: Run `git log --all --oneline -- 'docs/**/schemas/**'` to find whether schemas were ever committed. If they were and got deleted, restore or update the test path. If never created, delete the test or skip with reason.

drift-from-recent-refactor.description: agent-contracts kernel size budget: product kernel grew beyond the generalist target. Recent additions to the product agent prompt pushed it over the byte budget enforced by tests/agent-contracts.test.js.
files: tests/agent-contracts.test.js:229:product, sheldon, and dev kernels use deterministic on-demand docs and stay within size targets (product kernel oversize)
drift-from-recent-refactor.fix_recipe: Decision: either (a) raise the product kernel byte budget in tests/agent-contracts.test.js (acknowledge prompt growth) or (b) trim the product agent .md to fit. Not a tester decision.

real-assertion-failure-singleton.status: FIXED 2026-05-14 (@dev) — 2 real singletons + 1 absorbed by bug-005
real-assertion-failure-singleton.description: Two of the three singletons share a common Windows path-separator root cause: production code uses path.join which emits backslash on Windows; tests asserted forward-slash substrings. The third singleton (live-command.test.js:308) was misclassified — it was part of the live-cluster ENOENT chain and got fixed by bug-005.
files: tests/agent-teams-adapter.test.js:122:FIXED (executorToTeammate now normalizes to forward-slash)
tests/learning-auto-promote.test.js:92:FIXED (promoted_items[0].file now normalized to forward-slash)
tests/live-command.test.js:308:FIXED via bug-005 (not a singleton; was symptomatic of state.json never written)
real-assertion-failure-singleton.resolution: Both real singletons fixed by normalizing path output to forward-slash after path.join in user-facing fields (.replace(/\\\\/g, '/')). Decision: normalize at production boundary (not in tests) because these fields are emitted in JSON outputs, CLI logs, and consumed by downstream automation across platforms. Mixed separators would be an interop problem. agent-teams-adapter 36/36 + learning-auto-promote 10/10 green. Full suite: 2414/2422.

perf-flake-windows-io.description: QA-PERF-01 — p99 latency stays under 100ms across 500 sequential context:load calls. Already documented as Windows-IO-sensitive flake. Most recent run measured p99 = 1047.99ms (10× SLA), which is unusual even for a flake — may be system load at the time of run.
files: tests/qa-telemetry-foundation.test.js:30:QA-PERF-01: p99 latency stays under 100ms across 500 sequential context:load calls
perf-flake-windows-io.fix_recipe: Options: (a) raise the SLA for Windows (e.g., 250ms p99), (b) skip on Windows entirely with platform check, (c) leave as-is and accept the flake. Decision belongs to @architect.
